homework.title: Decimal Expansion of Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Find the decimal expansion of the following rational numbers. State whether they have a terminating or non-terminating repeating decimal expansion.
homework.answerSpace: Write the decimal value and classify it as 'Terminating' or 'Non-terminating Repeating'.

homework.title: Understanding Rational Numbers on the Number Line
homework.instructions: Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) carefully. Choose the correct option from the choices given below.
homework.question: Assertion (A): Every rational number can be represented as a point on the number line. Reason (R): Rational numbers are of the form p/q where q is not zero.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a number line showing the position of 3/4 and -2/5 to support your reasoning if needed.
homework.expectedResponse: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A

homework.title: Real-World Application of Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Solve the following real-life problem using rational numbers.
homework.question: A tank is filled by two pipes. Pipe A fills 1/3 of the tank in one hour, and Pipe B fills 1/4 of the tank in one hour. If both pipes are opened together, what fraction of the tank will be filled in one hour?
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a simple diagram representing the tank and the two pipes filling it.
homework.expectedResponse: 1/3 + 1/4 = 4/12 + 3/12 = 7/12. The tank will be filled by 7/12 in one hour.

homework.title: Operations with Rational Numbers
topicCoverage: Addition and Subtraction of Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Perform the following operations and simplify your answer.
homework.answerSpace: Show the common denominator method used.

homework.title: Analyzing Patterns in Decimal Expansions
homework.instructions: Read the case study and answer the questions below.
homework.question: Case: A student observes that when dividing 1 by prime numbers like 7, 13, etc., the decimal expansion repeats. However, when dividing by 2 or 5, it terminates. Question: Explain why the denominator's prime factors determine whether a rational number has a terminating or non-terminating repeating decimal expansion.
homework.visualRequirement: Create a small table comparing denominators 2, 5, 7, and 13 with their decimal types.
homework.expectedResponse: If the prime factorization of the denominator contains only 2s and/or 5s, the decimal terminates. If it contains any other prime factor, the decimal repeats.

learningOutcomesCovered: LO_3.1: Identify terminating and non-terminating decimal expansions
LO_3.2: Represent rational numbers on the number line
LO_3.3: Apply rational numbers to real-world problems
LO_3.4: Perform addition and subtraction of rational numbers
LO_3.5: Analyze patterns in decimal expansions
topicsCovered: Addition and Subtraction of Rational Numbers
